subject: Geography
title: Antarctic Research Stations
description: A quick win: understand Antarctic Research Stations.

hook.text: Over 70 research stations operate in Antarctica, staffed by scientists from 30 countries.

buildup.text: McMurdo Station is the largest—home to 1,000 people in summer with its own airport and harbor.

discovery.text: Scientists drill ice cores that contain air bubbles from 800,000 years ago—a climate time machine.

twist.text: Winter crews endure months of darkness, isolation, and temperatures below minus 80°C.

climax.text: Antarctic research has revealed the ozone hole, ancient climates, and life's extreme limits.

punchline.text: The loneliest labs on Earth answer the biggest questions about it.

quiz.question: What do Antarctic ice cores reveal?
options: Climate conditions from up to 800,000 years ago
The age of the continent itself
